benchmark_details.name: AVHBench (Audio-Visual Hallucination Benchmark)
benchmark_details.overview: AVHBench is a comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate the perception and comprehension capabilities of audio-visual LLMs, focusing on their susceptibility to cross-modal hallucinations.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To assess the perception and comprehension capabilities of audio-visual LLMs, specifically that pertaining to hallucinations caused by cross-interactions between audio and visual signals.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: Although the benchmark aims to assess cross-modal hallucinations, it may not encompass all possible scenarios due to the complexity of multimodal signals.

data.source: The dataset comprises real and synthetic source videos collected from existing datasets, namely VALOR and AudioCaps.
data.size: 10,327 videos with 87,624 QnA pairs and 1,106 audio-visual captions
data.annotation: A semi-automatic annotation pipeline with human validation is employed.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are calculated based on both judgment and description tasks using standard definitions.
methodology.interpretation: High accuracy and F1 scores indicate better model performance in discerning between genuine and hallucinated signals.
methodology.baseline_results: Models evaluated on AVHBench are X-InstructBLIP, ImageBind-LLM, Video-LLaMA, ChatBridge, PandaGPT, and OneLLM.
methodology.validation: The benchmark's reliability is ensured through a comprehensive human verification process in the dataset construction.

targeted_risks.harm: The benchmark aims to detect and mitigate hallucinations in audio-visual LLMs.
